item.title: Member Engagement Ideas to Boost Retention and Grow Your Association
Best Credentialing Software in 2026: Features, Reviews & Pricing
item.content:encoded:
Managing credentials is one of the most operationally demanding responsibilities an organization can take on. Whether you're overseeing professional certifications, occupational licenses, continuing education (CE) requirements, or compliance-driven renewals, the underlying complexity is significant.  

Credential programs must track individuals across multiple credential types, monitor expiration dates, administer exams, enforce eligibility rules, and maintain audit-ready records—all while staying aligned with evolving regulatory and accreditation standards. For associations, certification bodies, healthcare systems, and other credentialing organizations, manual methods introduce complexity. 

Generic tools, spreadsheets, shared calendars, even broad-purpose databases, consistently fall short. Thes tools lack the automated workflows needed to catch upcoming renewals before they lapse, the structured data models required for multi-step exam management, and the reporting infrastructure that audits and accreditation reviews demand. Manual data reconciliation leads to missed renewal deadlines, compliance gaps, and multiple staff hours lost. That's why organizations looking for the best credentialing software turn to purpose-built platforms designed specifically for the credential lifecycle. 

Purpose-built credentialing software centralizes the entire credential lifecycle in a single system—from application and eligibility verification through exam administration, issuance, renewal tracking, and continuing education management. These platforms replace fragmented manual processes with automated workflows, configurable rules of engines, and integrated communications, so credential programs can operate efficiently at any scale. 

This guide covers the best credentialing software options available in 2026, organized by use case and buyer profile. For each platform, we've included an overview, key features, reviews, pricing, and our reasoning for the selection, so you can evaluate options against your program's specific requirements. 

What Is Credentialing Software? 

Credentialing software is designed to manage the full lifecycle of credentials, certifications, and licenses — for individuals, member populations, or regulated workforces. At its core, a credentialing system tracks who holds what credential, under what conditions, and with what expiration or renewal requirements. Purpose-built platforms also handle exam administration, continuing education logging, digital badge issuance, compliance documentation, and integration with external systems like association management software (AMS) or learning management systems (LMS). 

Certification management software is a closely related term — and in many contexts, the two are used interchangeably. The distinction, where one exists, is typically one of scope: credentialing software is often associated with regulated industries (healthcare, professional licensing) where compliance and verification are paramount, while certification management software is more commonly used by associations and professional bodies managing voluntary certification programs. In practice, leading platforms serve both use cases. 

When evaluating credentialing software, organizations should assess platforms against the following criteria: 

  • Automated renewal tracking and notifications — proactive alerts that prevent lapses without manual follow-up 
  • Exam and continuing education (CE) management — support for eligibility rules, exam scheduling, CE activity logging, and credit tracking 
  • Compliance and audit trail capabilities — complete, timestamped records of all credential actions for accreditation reviews and regulatory requirements 
  • Integration with AMS, LMS, or member databases — bidirectional data exchange to eliminate duplicate records and manual syncing 
  • Scalability across credential types and volumes — the ability to manage multiple distinct credentials simultaneously without rebuilding workflows from scratch 

          What Is the Best Credentialing Software? 

          The best credentialing software depends heavily on your organization's type, program complexity, credential volume, and integration requirements. Below we've organized top platforms by the buyer profile they serve best — from enterprise-grade solutions for large associations to accessible tools for smaller or budget-constrained programs. 

          Here is a list of the best credentialing software:  

          • Best Credentialing Software Overall — Momentive Certification Management 
          • Best for Associations and Certification Bodies — Credly (Pearson) 
          • Best for Healthcare Credentialing — Symplr Credentialing 
          • Best for Continuing Education (CE) Management — Novi AMS with CE Module 
          • Best for Small Credentialing Programs — Certemy 
          • Best Free or Low-Cost Credentialing Software — Accredible 

          Best Credentialing Software Overall

          Momentive Certification Management 

          Overview

          Momentive's Certification Management platform is purpose-built for associations and certification bodies that need to manage the complete credential lifecycle — from application intake and eligibility verification through exam administration, CE tracking, digital credentialing, and renewal management. Unlike horizontal platforms that require heavy configuration to approximate credentialing workflows, Momentive is built around the operational model of professional certification programs from the ground up. 

          Key Features 

          • End-to-end credential lifecycle management, from application to renewal 
          • Automated renewal tracking with configurable notification sequences 
          • Exam eligibility management with custom rules engines 
          • Continuing education (CE) activity logging and credit management 
          • Digital badge and certificate issuance 
          • Compliance-ready audit trails and reporting 
          • Native integration with AMS and member database systems 
          • Multi-credential support for organizations managing several distinct certifications 
          • Candidate and certificant self-service portal 

                            Reviews 

                            Users consistently highlight the platform's depth of credentialing-specific functionality and the quality of implementation support. Associations with complex, multi-step certification programs note that Momentive handles operational scenarios that generic tools cannot accommodate without significant workarounds. Common praise centers on the renewal automation workflows, the audit trail completeness, and the native AMS connectivity that eliminates the data-sync overhead common with third-party integrations. 

                            Benefits 

                            • Eliminates the configuration burden of adapting horizontal software to certification workflows 
                            • Reduces staff time spent on renewal follow-up through automated notification sequences 
                            • Provides audit-ready records that support accreditation reviews without manual report assembly 
                            • Scales across credential types without requiring separate systems per certification 
                            • Tight AMS integration maintains a single source of truth for member and certificant data 

                                    Pricing 

                                    Momentive offers custom pricing based on organization size, credential volume, and feature configuration. Contact Momentive directly for a tailored quote. 

                                    Why We Picked It 

                                    Momentive Certification Management earns the overall top position because it combines the deepest credentialing-specific feature set with purpose-built architecture for the association and certification body market. It doesn't require organizations to build credentialing workflows on top of a generic platform, the workflows are native. For any organization whose primary operational challenge is managing professional certifications at scale, Momentive is the purpose-built answer. 
                                     

                                    [elementor-template id="1526"]

                                    Best for Associations and Certification Bodies 

                                    Credly (Pearson) 

                                    Overview 

                                    Credly, now part of Pearson, is a leading digital credentialing platform focused on the issuance, management, and verification of digital badges and credentials. Its strength lies in the visibility and portability it gives credential holders — issued credentials are shareable to LinkedIn, verifiable by employers, and discoverable within Credly's network. For associations that want to extend the value of their certification programs through digital credential recognition, Credly provides strong infrastructure. 

                                    Key Features 

                                    • Digital badge issuance and management at scale 
                                    • Credential verification for employers and third parties 
                                    • Analytics on credential engagement and earner sharing behavior 
                                    • Integration with LMS and AMS platforms 
                                    • Credly's open badge network for credential discoverability 
                                    • Earner-facing credential wallet and sharing tools 

                                              Reviews 

                                              Users appreciate Credly's ease of use for issuing badges and the platform's reputation as the dominant network for digital credential sharing. Reviews note that the learner experience is polished, and that LinkedIn integration drives meaningful visibility for certification programs. Some users note that Credly functions better as a digital issuance layer than as a comprehensive program management system. 

                                              Benefits 

                                              • Elevates the perceived value of association credentials through a recognized digital format 
                                              • Earner-driven sharing increases program visibility without additional marketing spend 
                                              • Third-party verification infrastructure reduces the manual verification burden on staff 
                                              • Backed by Pearson's credentialing ecosystem 

                                                    Pricing 

                                                    Credly pricing is volume-based and available by contacting Pearson/Credly directly. Enterprise agreements are available for large programs. 

                                                    Why We Picked It 

                                                    For associations that want to amplify the reach and recognizability of their credentials, Credly's network effect and employer verification infrastructure make it a strong choice. It's best positioned as a digital credentialing layer — ideally paired with a program management platform like Momentive for end-to-end lifecycle management. 

                                                    Best for Healthcare Credentialing 

                                                    Symplr Credentialing 

                                                    Overview 

                                                    Symplr Credentialing is purpose-built for healthcare organizations managing provider credentialing and privileging — a high-stakes, high-compliance workflow covering license verification, primary source verification (PSV), privileging decisions, and ongoing monitoring. Healthcare credentialing carries distinct regulatory requirements under The Joint Commission, NCQA, and CMS that general credentialing platforms are not designed to meet. Symplr is built around these requirements. 

                                                    Key Features 

                                                    • Primary source verification (PSV) automation 
                                                    • Provider license and DEA number tracking 
                                                    • Privileging workflow management 
                                                    • Continuous monitoring and sanctions screening (OIG, SAM, state boards) 
                                                    • Joint Commission and NCQA-aligned reporting 
                                                    • Credentialing committee workflow support 
                                                    • Integration with HRIS and EHR platforms 

                                                                Reviews 

                                                                Healthcare credentialing professionals note that Symplr significantly reduces the time required to complete provider credentialing files compared to manual processes. The compliance reporting capabilities are frequently cited as a strength, particularly for organizations preparing for Joint Commission surveys. Some reviewers note that the platform has a learning curve and that implementation timelines should be planned carefully. 

                                                                Benefits 

                                                                • Automates the most time-intensive elements of healthcare provider credentialing 
                                                                • Built-in compliance alignment with major accreditation bodies 
                                                                • Continuous monitoring reduces risk exposure between credentialing cycles 
                                                                • Reduces credentialing turnaround time for new provider onboarding 

                                                                      Pricing 

                                                                      Symplr pricing is enterprise-negotiated. Contact Symplr for a quote based on provider volume and facility count. 

                                                                      Why We Picked It 

                                                                      Healthcare credentialing requirements are sufficiently distinct — in compliance demands, workflow complexity, and regulatory stakes — to warrant a dedicated category. Symplr is one of the most established platforms built specifically for provider credentialing and privileging in healthcare settings. 

                                                                      Best for Continuing Education (CE) Management

                                                                      Novi AMS with CE Module 

                                                                      Overview 

                                                                      For organizations where continuing education management is the primary credentialing workflow — tracking activity completion, managing CE credit requirements, and connecting education records to certification status — Novi AMS offers a tightly integrated solution. Its CE management capabilities sit within a broader association management context, making it well-suited to associations that run both membership and credentialing programs on a unified platform. Continuing education management software in this category is most valuable when CE and certification data need to stay synchronized without manual reconciliation. 

                                                                      Key Features 

                                                                      • CE activity logging and credit hour tracking 
                                                                      • Automated CE requirement enforcement tied to credential status 
                                                                      • CE transcript management and member-facing CE history 
                                                                      • Event and course integration for CE credit capture 
                                                                      • Renewal eligibility gating based on CE completion 
                                                                      • Reporting for accreditation and compliance purposes 

                                                                                Reviews 

                                                                                Users value the clean integration between CE tracking and membership records — eliminating the need for separate systems or manual data transfers. The member-facing CE transcript functionality receives positive mention, as it reduces inbound member support requests about CE history. Smaller associations particularly appreciate the all-in-one positioning. 

                                                                                Benefits 

                                                                                • Unifies CE management and membership administration in one system 
                                                                                • Automates CE-gated renewal eligibility enforcement 
                                                                                • Reduces member support burden through self-service CE history access 
                                                                                • Simplifies accreditation reporting with structured CE records 

                                                                                      Pricing 

                                                                                      Novi AMS pricing is tiered by member count. CE management is included in higher-tier plans. Contact Novi for current pricing. 

                                                                                      Why We Picked It 

                                                                                      Organizations where CE management is the dominant credentialing workflow — rather than exam-based certification — benefit most from a solution that connects CE records directly to membership and credential status. Novi's integrated approach serves this profile well. 

                                                                                      Best for Small Credentialing Programs

                                                                                      Certemy 

                                                                                      Overview 

                                                                                      Certemy is a credential management platform positioned for small to mid-sized organizations that need structured, automated credential tracking without the complexity or cost of enterprise-grade systems. It's particularly well-suited to workforce credentialing scenarios — HR teams, staffing firms, and small professional bodies tracking licenses and certifications across a staff or membership population. 

                                                                                      Key Features 

                                                                                      • License and certification tracking with automated expiration alerts 
                                                                                      • Primary source verification for regulated license types 
                                                                                      • Real-time credential status dashboards 
                                                                                      • Document storage and upload management 
                                                                                      • Configurable workflow automation for credential renewals 
                                                                                      • Compliance reporting and audit-ready record exports 

                                                                                                Reviews 

                                                                                                Small and mid-sized organizations rate Certemy well for its usability and the immediate reduction in manual tracking effort. The automated expiration alerts receive consistent praise as the most impactful feature. Some users note that advanced customization options are more limited compared to enterprise platforms. 

                                                                                                Benefits 

                                                                                                • Rapid implementation with low IT overhead 
                                                                                                • Eliminates spreadsheet-based tracking for credential-heavy HR teams 
                                                                                                • Affordable entry point for programs not yet at enterprise scale 
                                                                                                • Reduces compliance risk through proactive expiration management 

                                                                                                      Pricing 

                                                                                                      Certemy pricing starts at approximately $3–$5 per user per month for basic tracking tiers. Volume pricing is available. Contact Certemy for current plan details. 

                                                                                                      Why We Picked It 

                                                                                                      For organizations that are outgrowing spreadsheets but aren't ready for a full enterprise credentialing platform, Certemy provides structured automation at an accessible price point. It's the right step up for credential programs with straightforward tracking needs. 

                                                                                                      Best Free or Low-Cost Credentialing Software

                                                                                                      Accredible 

                                                                                                      Overview 

                                                                                                      Accredible is a digital certificate and badge platform with a free tier that makes it accessible to early-stage credential programs, small nonprofits, and organizations testing a digital credentialing initiative. Its focus is on digital credential issuance — generating, delivering, and verifying certificates and badges — rather than full program management. For programs that need a low-cost, polished way to issue digital credentials, Accredible is a practical starting point. 

                                                                                                      Key Features 

                                                                                                      • Free tier for up to a limited number of credential issuances per month 
                                                                                                      • Customizable digital certificate and badge templates 
                                                                                                      • Credential verification pages for each issued credential 
                                                                                                      • LinkedIn sharing integration 
                                                                                                      • Basic credential analytics (views, shares) 
                                                                                                      • CSV bulk issuance for batch credentialing 

                                                                                                                Reviews 

                                                                                                                Users praise Accredible's ease of setup and the professional quality of the issued certificates and badges. The free tier is widely regarded as genuinely useful — not just a trial. Organizations note that as their programs grow, the platform scales with paid tiers that add API access, white-labeling, and higher issuance volumes. 

                                                                                                                Benefits 

                                                                                                                • Zero cost to get started — meaningful for budget-constrained programs 
                                                                                                                • Professional-looking credentials without design overhead 
                                                                                                                • Quick implementation — most programs can issue first credentials within hours 
                                                                                                                • Scales to paid tiers as programs grow without platform migration 

                                                                                                                      Pricing 

                                                                                                                      Free tier available with limited monthly issuances. Paid plans begin at approximately $99/month and scale by volume. Enterprise pricing is available for high-volume programs. 

                                                                                                                      Why We Picked It 

                                                                                                                      Accredible's free tier and fast setup make it the most accessible entry point for organizations beginning a digital credentialing program. It doesn't offer the program management depth of enterprise platforms, but for pure credential issuance at low or no cost, it's the strongest option in the category. 

                                                                                                                      Choosing the Right Credentialing Software 

                                                                                                                      Selecting the right credentialing software comes down to three primary dimensions: your organization type, your credential volume and complexity, and your compliance requirements. A healthcare system managing ongoing provider privileging has materially different needs from a professional association issuing a single certification, and both differ from a small organization just beginning to formalize its credentialing processes. 

                                                                                                                      Use the evaluation checklist from the "What Is Credentialing Software?" section to pressure-test any platform you're considering:  

                                                                                                                      • Does it automate renewal tracking and notifications?  
                                                                                                                      • Does it support exam and CE management natively?  
                                                                                                                      • Does it maintain audit-ready compliance records?  
                                                                                                                      • Can it integrate with your AMS or member database?  
                                                                                                                      • Will it scale as your program grows? 

                                                                                                                      For associations and certification bodies, the core market for professional credentialing software, Momentive Certification Management stands out as the purpose-built choice. It's the only platform on this list designed specifically for the end-to-end certification lifecycle that associations operate: managing applicants, enforcing eligibility, administering exams, tracking CE, issuing credentials, and managing renewals across potentially tens of thousands of certificants. If your organization's operational core is running a professional certification program, Momentive is built for exactly that mission. 

                                                                                                                      [elementor-template id="1458"]

                                                                                                                      FAQs 

                                                                                                                      What Is the Best Credentialing Software? 

                                                                                                                      The best credentialing software overall is Momentive Certification Management, particularly for associations and professional certification bodies that need to manage the full credential lifecycle. For healthcare credentialing, Symplr is the leading purpose-built solution. For small programs or budget-constrained organizations, Certemy and Accredible offer accessible entry points. The best choice depends on your organization type, credential volume, and compliance requirements. 

                                                                                                                      What Is Credentialing Software Used For?

                                                                                                                      Credentialing software is used to manage the complete lifecycle of credentials, certifications, and licenses within an organization or for an external population. Use cases include tracking certification status and renewal dates, administering exams and eligibility requirements, logging continuing education activity and credits, issuing digital badges and certificates, maintaining compliance documentation and audit trails, and verifying credential status for employers or regulators. 

                                                                                                                      What Features Should Credentialing Software Have? 

                                                                                                                      At minimum, credentialing software should include automated renewal tracking with proactive notifications, exam and CE management, compliance-ready audit trail capabilities, integration with AMS or LMS platforms, and the ability to scale across multiple credential types and volumes. More advanced programs may also require primary source verification, digital badge issuance, exam eligibility rules engines, and candidate self-service portals. 

                                                                                                                      What Is the Difference Between Credentialing Software and Certification Management Software? 

                                                                                                                      The terms are often used interchangeably, but a distinction sometimes exists in industry usage. Credentialing software is frequently associated with regulated environments, particularly healthcare, where the emphasis is on compliance verification, primary source verification, and regulatory alignment. Certification management software is more commonly used by associations and professional bodies managing voluntary certification programs with exam-based eligibility, CE requirements, and renewal cycles. In practice, leading platforms serve both use cases. 

                                                                                                                      How Much Does Credentialing Software Cost?

                                                                                                                      Credentialing software pricing varies significantly by platform, program size, and feature tier. Entry-level or digital issuance-focused tools like Accredible offer free tiers with paid plans beginning around $99/month. Workforce-focused platforms like Certemy typically start at $3–$5 per user per month. Enterprise platforms such as Momentive Certification Management and Symplr use custom pricing based on credential volume and organizational scope. Most enterprise-tier credentialing platforms do not publish list pricing and require a direct quote.

item.title: Best Nonprofit Software in 2026: Features, Reviews & Pricing
item.content:encoded:
Running a nonprofit is different than running any ordinary business. Yes, there are budgets to balance and stakeholders to satisfy—but there are also grant compliance requirements, donor stewardship cycles, membership renewals, volunteer coordination, program delivery, and annual audits, often managed by a small team with limited resources and an enormous sense of purpose. The tools that work well for a sales-driven company rarely account for any of that. 

The best nonprofit software is built differently. It's designed around the actual workflows of mission-driven organizations—from fundraising campaigns and event management to member engagement, fund accounting, learning programs, and volunteer coordination. Rather than forcing nonprofits to adapt to tools built for other industries, purpose-built platforms adapt to them. 

That's why so many nonprofits eventually hit a wall with general business software. A CRM designed for B2B pipelines doesn't understand fund accounting. A project management tool doesn't track donor giving history or send tax acknowledgment letters. Spreadsheets can only go so far before they become a liability rather than an asset. The workarounds multiply, the manual effort compounds, and the mission gets crowded out by operational noise. 

This guide covers the leading options across the categories nonprofits care about most, with practical guidance on what to look for, how to evaluate your options, and which platforms are worth your time. 

What Is Nonprofit Software? 

Nonprofit software is a broad category of technology platforms designed specifically to support the operational, financial, and engagement needs of nonprofit organizations, associations, and other mission-driven entities. This software spans several distinct functional areas: accounting and financial management, fundraising and donor engagement, association and membership management (AMS), event planning and execution, volunteer coordination, and learning and credentialing programs. Some organizations need one well. Others need most of them—and need them to work together. 

When evaluating nonprofit software, consider the following: 

  • Mission-specific functionality vs. general business tools. Does the platform understand how nonprofits operate, or are you retrofitting a commercial product? 
  • Integration across departments and platforms. Can your fundraising data connect to your accounting? Can your AMS share data with your events and learning platforms? 
  • Scalability as programs and membership grow. Will the platform support your organization at twice its current size without requiring a full technology overhaul? 
  • Compliance, reporting, and audit readiness. Does the software produce the financial reports and documentation your auditors, board, and funders expect? 
  • Support and implementation tailored to nonprofits. Is the vendor's team familiar with the sector? Do they offer real implementation support, not just a knowledge base? 

What Is the Best Nonprofit Software? 

There's no single answer — the right nonprofit software depends on your organization's size, structure, and primary programs. Below, we've organized our picks by use case so you can find the best fit for your specific needs. 

  • Best Nonprofit Software Overall — Momentive Software 
  • Best Nonprofit Accounting Software — MIP Accounting 
  • Best Nonprofit CRM and Donor Management Software — Donor CRM   
  • Best Nonprofit Fundraising Software — GiveSmart 
  • Best Volunteer Management Software — Volunteer Matters
  • Best Matching Gift Software — Double the Donation
[elementor-template id="1458"]

Best Nonprofit Software Overall 

Momentive Software 

Overview 

Momentive Software offers a comprehensive nonprofit software platform designed to help mission-driven organizations manage more, with tools for fundraising, membership, events, fund accounting, learning, volunteer management, and donor engagement all in one connected ecosystem. 

Built for organizations of any size, it streamlines operations, eliminates the cost and complexity of managing disconnected tools, and helps teams focus on what matters most—advancing their mission and growing their impact. 

Key Features 

  • Unified platform spanning fundraising, AMS, events, fund accounting, learning, certification, and volunteer management 
  • MomentiveIQ AI layer for automated workflows, predictive engagement, and data-driven insights across every solution 
  • Fund accounting built for nonprofit compliance, grant management, and audit readiness 
  • Association management tools for the full membership lifecycle, across recruitment, renewal, chapters, and credentialing 
  • Dedicated implementation services, live support, and sector-specific expertise 

Reviews 

Nonprofit professionals across sectors value Momentive Software for the breadth of its capabilities, the quality of its implementation support, and the reliability of its technology. Organizations consistently highlight the time saved when teams stop managing data across multiple platforms and start working with a single vendor. 

Benefits 

  • Eliminates the cost and complexity of managing multiple disconnected tools 
  • Improves data integrity across every department—fundraising, membership, finance, and events 
  • Enables smarter engagement through AI-driven insights and automation 
  • Scales from small nonprofits to large, complex associations without requiring a platform change 
  • Backed by implementation and support teams with deep nonprofit sector expertise 

Pricing 

Momentive Software offers tailored pricing based on your organization's size, structure, and the combination of solutions you need. Contact the team for a custom quote. 

Why We Picked It 

We recommend Momentive Software for nonprofits seeking a unified platform that connects every part of their operations—from fundraising and membership to accounting and learning—without the overhead of managing a patchwork of tools. 

Request a demo to see how Momentive Software can help your organization bring on better. 

Best Nonprofit Accounting Software 

MIP Accounting by Momentive Software 

Overview 

MIP Accounting is purpose-built nonprofit accounting software designed to help organizations manage more with tools for fund-based financials, grant tracking, multi-entity reporting, and audit documentation—all in one platform. 

Built for nonprofits of any size, it eliminates the manual reconciliation required when using general business accounting tools and helps finance teams focus on what matters most—accurate reporting, clean audits, and confident financial decisions. 

Key Features 

  • Fund accounting with full segregation of restricted, unrestricted, and temporarily restricted funds 
  • Grant management, expenditure tracking, and funder reporting 
  • Audit-ready financial reporting and Form 990 support 
  • Multi-entity and multi-fund management for complex organizational structures 
  • Direct integration with nonprofit fundraising for end-to-end revenue tracking 
  • Ability to integrate with third-party software 

Reviews 

Nonprofit finance teams value MIP Accounting for its reliability, audit trail completeness, and the ability to produce the reports that boards and funders actually need. Users consistently highlight how much time is saved when fundraising data flows automatically into fund accounting, eliminating manual reconciliation and the errors that come with it. 

Benefits 

  • Maintains compliance with nonprofit accounting standards (FASB ASC 958) 
  • Reduces audit preparation time with complete, organized financial documentation 
  • Gives board and leadership real-time visibility into organizational financial health 
  • Connects directly to fundraising data—no manual reconciliation required 
  • Scales to support growth without requiring a move to a different platform 
  • Provides AI-powered dashboards for easy analysis and reporting 

Pricing 

MIP Accounting offers subscription-based pricing tailored to your entity count, user needs, and module requirements. Contact the team for a custom quote. 

Why We Picked It 

Nonprofit accounting is too consequential to manage in a general business tool. MIP Accounting is built specifically for the fund-based financial structures that nonprofits operate under, and because it connects directly to GiveSmart’s nonprofit fundraising within the Momentive platform, finance teams get a complete, connected picture of every dollar raised and spent. For a full comparison of accounting options, see our guide to the best nonprofit accounting software. 

Best Nonprofit CRM and Donor Management Software 

Donor CRM by Momentive Software 

Overview 

DonorCRM offers comprehensive donor management and CRM software designed to help nonprofits build stronger relationships and raise more with tools for giving history, donor segmentation, stewardship workflows, and campaign analytics all in one platform. 

Built for organizations of any size, it eliminates the gaps that come from managing donor data in disconnected systems and helps development teams focus on what matters most—cultivating relationships and growing revenue. 

Key Features 

  • Complete donor profiles with full giving history, communication records, and engagement tracking 
  • Donor segmentation and targeted outreach tools for campaigns and stewardship 
  • Automated acknowledgment letters, tax receipts, and recurring giving workflows 
  • Enhanced dashboard and reporting on donors, campaigns, events, and pipeline 
  • Excellent extended-hours customer service, implementation services, and live support 

Reviews 

Nonprofit development professionals value DonorCRM for its ease of use, reliability, and the way it connects donor data to every other part of their fundraising operation. Users consistently highlight the time saved when stewardship, acknowledgment, and reporting are automated, and the confidence that comes from having a complete, accurate picture of every donor. 

Benefits 

  • Builds stronger donor relationships through timely, personalized stewardship 
  • Increases retention by surfacing at-risk donors before they lapse 
  • Connects donor engagement data directly to fundraising campaigns and fund accounting 
  • Unlimited campaigns, events, and users at scale without costly add-ons 
  • Saves administrative time with an award-winning all-in-one platform 

Pricing 

Donor CRM offers one comprehensive plan with all features, unlimited users, and unlimited campaigns included. Donor CRM does not charge transaction or ticketing fees — no à la carte packages or surprise add-on costs. 

Why We Picked It 

We recommend Donor CRM by Momentive Software for nonprofits looking for donor management and CRM capabilities that connect directly to fundraising, events, and fund accounting—giving development teams a complete view of every supporter and every dollar. Request a demo to see how Donor CRM can help your team bring on better for your donors and mission. 

Best Nonprofit Fundraising Software 

GiveSmart by Momentive Software 

Overview 

GiveSmart offers comprehensive fundraising software designed to help nonprofits manage more with tools for donations, events, auctions, peer-to-peer campaigns, and donor data all in one platform. 

Built for organizations of any size, it streamlines fundraising management, eliminates manual work, and helps teams focus on what matters most—growing impact and engagement. 

Key Features 

  • Customizable and mobile-friendly fundraising pages that engage donors with auctions, raffles, sales, paddle raises, golf events, GivingTuesday, recurring giving, and more 
  • Peer-to-peer fundraising for individuals and teams, with easy sign-ups and unique text-to-donate keywords 
  • Enhanced dashboard and reporting on donations, donors, events, and campaigns 
  • Excellent extended-hours customer service, implementation services, live event support, and a help center that ensures your team makes a bigger impact on your mission 

Reviews 

Nonprofit professionals value GiveSmart for its ease of use, security, reliability, and responsive support team. Users consistently mention the time savings gained from managing multiple fundraising activities in one system and the peace of mind that comes with dependable technology. 

Benefits 

  • Streamlines complex fundraising workflows across events, campaigns, and donor management 
  • Enhances donor engagement through secure, easy digital wallet payments, interactive, and mobile-friendly experiences 
  • Increases fundraising with data-driven insights and automated tools 
  • Unlimited ticketing, campaigns, recurring giving, events, and users allow your team to scale fundraising without costly add-ons and additional tools 
  • Saves administrative time and reduces costs by offering an award-winning all-in-one fundraising platform 

Pricing 

GiveSmart offers one comprehensive fundraising plan, with all features, unlimited users, and unlimited campaigns and events included. GiveSmart does not charge transaction or ticketing fees, with no complicated à la carte packages or extra fees. 

Why We Picked It 

We recommend GiveSmart by Momentive Software for nonprofits, associations, and schools looking for an all-in-one fundraising and donor management platform that empowers better fundraising. Request a demo to see how GiveSmart can modernize your fundraising and help you bring on better for your donors and mission. 

Best Volunteer Management Software 

Volunteer Matters 

Overview 

Volunteer Matters by Momentive is volunteer management software designed to help nonprofits manage more with tools for recruitment, scheduling, hour tracking, communication, and recognition all in one connected system. 

Built for organizations of any size, it streamlines volunteer coordination, eliminates the manual overhead of managing programs across disconnected tools, and helps teams focus on what matters most—engaging their volunteers and expanding their community impact. 

Key Features 

  • Volunteer recruitment, background checking, onboarding, and profile management 
  • Scheduling, shift management, and automated reminders 
  • Hour tracking and impact reporting for grant documentation 
  • Recognition programs and engagement communications 
  • Integration with donor management to identify and cultivate volunteer-donors 

Reviews 

Nonprofit professionals managing volunteer programs value platforms that reduce the coordination overhead of scheduling and communication, make it easy for volunteers to self-manage their profiles and hours, and give program managers clear visibility into engagement and impact. The connection between volunteer data and donor records is a particularly high-value feature for development teams. 

Benefits 

  • Reduces administrative time spent on volunteer scheduling and communication 
  • Improves volunteer retention through recognition and consistent engagement 
  • Produces the hour tracking and impact data needed for grant reporting 
  • Surfaces volunteer-donors who may be candidates for deeper engagement and major gift cultivation 
  • Connects volunteer engagement to the broader organizational picture—donor management, programs, and accounting 

Pricing 

Volunteer Matters is available as part of Momentive Software's connected platform. Contact the team for pricing details based on your organization's needs. 

Why We Picked It 

Volunteer management works best when volunteer data connects to the rest of your organization. Volunteer Matters’ approach integrates volunteer engagement into the same platform as fundraising, donor management, and membership, giving organizations a complete picture of how their supporters show up for the mission. Request a demo to see how it can bring on better for your volunteer program. 

Best Matching Gift Software 

Double the Donation 

Overview 

Double the Donation helps nonprofits increase revenue by simplifying the corporate matching gift process. Its tools make it easy for donors to determine if their employer offers matching donations and automate the submission process, significantly boosting matched gift revenue with minimal administrative effort. 

Key Features 

  • Automated employer matching gift database and search 
  • Step-by-step guidance for donors to complete match requests 
  • Integrations with major donation and CRM platforms, including GiveSmart 
  • Real-time tracking of match submission status 

Reviews 

Nonprofits consistently praise Double the Donation for increasing their matching gift revenue and saving staff time. Many users highlight how automation replaces manual tracking and follow-up, allowing fundraising teams to focus on donor engagement instead of administrative work. 

Benefits 

  • Increases revenue by unlocking untapped matching funds 
  • Reduces manual data entry and repetitive outreach 
  • Enhances donor experience through automated guidance and confirmation 

Pricing 

Double the Donation offers three plans:  Essentials: For organizations with less than $500,000 in revenue, fees from $300–$1,200 annually Standard: Offering the best in functionality, this enhanced plan starts at $999 annually Enterprise: For large organizations requiring advanced features and customization, starting at $30,000 annually 

Why We Picked It 

Double the Donation is an excellent add-on for nonprofits focused on maximizing matching gift revenue. Many organizations will find this integration available within a connected platform like Momentive Software — eliminating the need for a separate subscription. 

Choosing the Right Nonprofit Software 

The right nonprofit software depends on where your organization is, what it needs right now, and where it's headed. A small community nonprofit with a focused fundraising program has different needs than a large national association managing thousands of members, a credentialing program, and an annual conference. Start with your most pressing operational challenge—whether that's disjointed financial reporting, fragmented donor data, or overworked staff managing manual processes—and evaluate platforms based on how well they solve that problem while leaving room to grow. 

For nonprofits and associations that are ready to move beyond the patchwork approach, Momentive Software offers the most complete connected platform in the sector. From fundraising and donor management to association management, accounting, learning, and volunteer coordination, it's built to bring on better outcomes for mission-driven organizations at every stage of growth. 

FAQ 

What Is the Best Software for Nonprofits?

The best option depends on your needs. Momentive Software offers an all-in-one platform, while GiveSmart is ideal for fundraising, combining events, auctions, peer-to-peer campaigns, and donor management in one system. Request a demo to learn more.

What Software Do Most Nonprofits Use? 

Nonprofits use tools for accounting, donor management, CRM, membership, and fundraising—often across multiple systems. Integrated platforms like Momentive Software help streamline operations and improve data consistency.

Is There Free Software for Nonprofits?

Some tools offer free or discounted plans, but they often have limitations. While options like BetterWorld provide low-cost entry, most nonprofits benefit more from paid, purpose-built software for scalability and full functionality.

What Is the Difference Between Nonprofit Software and Regular Business Software? 

Nonprofit software supports fundraising, grants, volunteers, and compliance, while business software focuses on sales and revenue. It’s purpose-built for mission-driven workflows that general business tools don’t handle well.

How Do I Choose the Right Nonprofit Software for My Organization? 

Identify your key pain points, then evaluate platforms on mission-specific features, integrations, scalability, compliance, and vendor support. Request demos, check references, and choose a solution that can grow with your organization.

item.title: Best Membership Management Software in 2026: Features, Reviews & Pricing
item.content:encoded:
Managing a membership organization is anything but simple. Between processing renewals, tracking dues, coordinating events, monitoring engagement, and keeping communications timely and personal, the operational demands can quickly outpace the tools most organizations rely on by default. Spreadsheets become unwieldy as membership rosters grow. Generic CRMs lack the built-in workflows that membership-centric processes require. What associations, nonprofits, and membership-based organizations need is purpose-built software designed specifically for the full member lifecycle. 

That’s where the best membership management software makes all the difference. These platforms are engineered to centralize member data, automate renewals and dues collection, streamline event registration, and surface the engagement analytics that help organizations grow and retain their communities. Rather than stitching together disconnected tools, purpose-built membership software brings every critical function under one roof. 

In this blog, we evaluate the top membership management platforms available in 2026 across several key categories, from best overall to best for small organizations and multi-chapter associations. Each entry covers core features, pricing, independent reviews, and a summary of what makes the platform stand out. Whether you are evaluating software for the first time or reconsidering your current stack, this breakdown will help you find the right fit. 

We’ve also included a practical buying guide to help you frame the decision based on your organization's size, membership model, and integration requirements. 

What Is Membership Management Software? 

Membership management software helps organizations manage the full lifecycle of their members—from onboarding and dues collection to engagement tracking, event management, and renewals. Unlike generic contact databases or CRMs, these platforms are designed around the specific workflows that membership organizations depend on daily. 

At their core, these purpose-built tools centralize member data, automate administrative tasks like renewal reminders and payment processing, and provide the reporting capabilities needed to understand member behavior and health across the organization. Many membership management solutions also support chapter management, online communities, and integrations with accounting, fundraising, and learning management systems. 

Core Capabilities to Look for in Membership Management Software: 

  • Member database and lifecycle tracking — a centralized record for every contact, with fields and workflows that map to membership stages from prospect to lapsed. 
  • Dues and renewal automation — automated billing cycles, renewal reminders, grace periods, and payment processing without manual intervention. 
  • Event and chapter management — tools to create, register, and track attendance for in-person and virtual events, with support for multi-chapter structures where relevant. 
  • Integration with accounting, fundraising, and LMS platforms — native or API-based connectors that allow membership data to flow cleanly into financial, fundraising, and educational systems. 
  • Reporting and engagement analytics — dashboards and export tools that surface renewal rates, engagement scores, attendance trends, and membership growth over time.

          What Is the Best Membership Management Software? 

          The right platform depends on your organization's size, structure, and specific needs. Below, we have identified the top solutions across six categories, from best overall to best for multi-chapter organizations, to help you identify the best fit quickly. 

          Here is a list of the best membership management software:   

          • Best Membership Management Software Overall — Momentive Software 
          • Best for Associations — Wild Apricot 
          • Best for Nonprofits — Bloomerang 
          • Best for Small Membership Organizations — MemberClicks 
          • Best for Multi-Chapter Organizations — Fonteva 
          • Best Free Membership Management Software — Member.io (Free Tier) 

          Best Membership Management Software Overall

          Momentive Software

          Momentive Software provides the best membership management software overall, offering a comprehensive suite of association management software solutions designed to serve associations and membership organizations at every stage of growth. With more than 30 years of association management software (AMS) experience and thousands of associations trusting its platform worldwide, Momentive delivers measurable results across member data, dues, events, communications, and analytics—all within a unified ecosystem. 

          What sets Momentive apart is its range of purpose-built products that scale to meet organizations wherever they are. Small-staff associations can leverage YourMembership for streamlined daily operations, while mid-size organizations benefit from NetForum's membership and operational tools. Larger and more complex associations can choose Cobalt, powered by Microsoft Dynamics 365, or Nimble AMS, built on Salesforce, for advanced analytics and automation. Aptify rounds out the portfolio for enterprise-level unions and associations requiring deep customization and scalability. 

          Key Features 

          • Member Management — attract, engage, and retain members with everything in one place, freeing staff to focus on member experience. 
          • Member Portal and E-Commerce — sell, collect, and manage dues, event registrations, donations, and merchandise in one seamless platform. 
          • Reporting and Analytics — real-time insights on membership growth, retention, engagement, and more through dashboards and reports. 
          • Event Management — manage everything from committee meetings to annual conferences, from planning through registration and reporting. 
          • Accounting and Financial Workflow — automate financial workflows and track detailed records associated with orders and transactions. 
          • CRM — centralize member data and turn every interaction into actionable insight instead of juggling multiple systems. 
          • Automation and AI  — boost staff productivity by automating routine tasks and surfacing predictive insights across your technology ecosystem. 
          • Member Engagement — build trust and community with personalized messaging tools. 
          • Professional Services — expert implementation, optimization, and ongoing support from a dedicated partner. 

                      AMS Products for Every Organization Size 

                      Pricing 

                      Momentive Software pricing is customized based on organization size, selected product, and feature requirements. Contact Momentive's team for a tailored quote or request a demo to explore the right solution for your association. 

                      Why We Picked It 

                      No other vendor in the market offers the same breadth of purpose-built AMS products designed to serve associations at every scale. Momentive's portfolio approach means organizations don't outgrow their software; they move to the next solution within the same trusted ecosystem.  
                       
                      With 30+ years of AMS expertise, deep integrations across fundraising, events, learning management, accounting, and certification, and an AI-powered intelligence layer through MomentiveIQ, Momentive delivers the most complete platform for associations that need more than basic contact management. 

                      [elementor-template id="1458"]

                      Best for Associations

                      Wild Apricot

                      Overview 

                      Wild Apricot is one of the most widely adopted membership management platforms for associations and professional societies of all sizes. It covers core membership workflows with a clean interface that non-technical staff can manage without IT support. 

                      Key Features 

                      • Membership database with configurable fields and member portal 
                      • Online dues collection and automated renewals 
                      • Event management with online registration 
                      • Email marketing and newsletter tools 
                      • Website builder with member-only content gating 

                              Reviews 

                              Wild Apricot holds consistently high ratings among association administrators for ease of use and value. Users appreciate its self-service member portal and built-in website builder. Some reviewers note that advanced reporting and custom integrations require higher-tier plans. 

                              Benefits 

                              • Low learning curve for staff and volunteers 
                              • All essential association functions in one subscription 
                              • Mobile app for members and administrators 

                                  Pricing 

                                  Plans start at approximately $48/month for up to 100 contacts, scaling to several hundred dollars monthly for larger organizations. A free trial is available. 

                                  Best for Nonprofits

                                  Bloomerang

                                  Overview 

                                  Bloomerang is for nonprofits that need to manage donor and membership relationships in one place. Its donor retention focus and engagement timeline make it especially strong for organizations where membership and fundraising overlap significantly. 

                                  Key Features 

                                  • Donor and member database with engagement timeline 
                                  • Online donation forms and membership dues collection 
                                  • Retention rate dashboard and giving trend analytics 
                                  • Email marketing with segmentation and automation 
                                  • Volunteer and event management modules 

                                          Reviews 

                                          Nonprofits consistently highlight Bloomerang's intuitive interface and responsive customer support as key differentiators. The retention-focused dashboard helps organizations identify at-risk members and donors before they lapse. 

                                          Benefits 

                                          • Combines fundraising and membership management in a single platform 
                                          • Strong nonprofit sector focus with relevant default workflows 
                                          • Clean, modern interface that reduces staff training time 

                                              Pricing 

                                              Bloomerang pricing starts around $125/month based on database size. Nonprofit discounts are available. Custom pricing for larger organizations is provided on request. 

                                              Best for Small Membership Organizations 

                                              MemberClicks

                                              Overview 

                                              MemberClicks is designed specifically for small to mid-size associations and chambers of commerce that need an AMS without enterprise complexity or pricing. It balances functionality with affordability, making it a popular choice among trade associations and professional groups with lean staff teams. 

                                              Key Features 

                                              • Member directory and profile management 
                                              • Dues billing and online payment processing 
                                              • Event registration and management 
                                              • Email and communication tools 
                                              • Member portal and self-service tools 

                                                      Reviews 

                                                      Small associations praise MemberClicks for its right-sized feature set and responsive support. Users appreciate that it covers all the essentials without overwhelming administrators with features they will never use. Some note the reporting tools have room for improvement at smaller tiers. 

                                                      Benefits 

                                                      • Priced for organizations with smaller membership databases 
                                                      • Fast implementation timeline with hands-on onboarding support 
                                                      • Purpose-built for chambers and trade associations 

                                                          Pricing 

                                                          MemberClicks pricing starts at approximately $300/month for smaller organizations. Packages are tailored to organization type and membership volume. Request a quote for a full pricing breakdown. 

                                                          Best for Multi-Chapter Organizations 

                                                          Fonteva

                                                          Overview 

                                                          Fonteva, built natively on Salesforce, is engineered for associations and membership organizations that operate across multiple chapters, regions, or affiliates. Its Salesforce foundation means deep CRM capability, enterprise-grade reporting, and a robust ecosystem of third-party integrations. 

                                                          Key Features 

                                                          • Native Salesforce architecture for enterprise CRM depth 
                                                          • Multi-chapter and multi-affiliate management with tiered permissions 
                                                          • eCommerce, dues management, and subscription billing 
                                                          • Event management with complex ticketing and sponsorship tracking 
                                                          • Advanced reporting via Salesforce Einstein Analytics 

                                                                  Reviews 

                                                                  Fonteva receives strong marks from large associations managing complex structures. Users value the Salesforce ecosystem access and flexibility. Implementation complexity and cost are recurring considerations raised in reviews, making it better suited to well-resourced organizations. 

                                                                  Benefits 

                                                                  • Handles enterprise-scale membership operations with complex hierarchies 
                                                                  • Extensive integration ecosystem via Salesforce AppExchange 
                                                                  • Highly customizable to support unique organizational structures 

                                                                      Pricing 

                                                                      Fonteva pricing is enterprise-tier and available on request. Expect significant investment relative to smaller platforms, reflecting its Salesforce foundation and implementation complexity. 

                                                                      Best Free Membership Management Software

                                                                      Member.io (Free Tier)

                                                                      Overview 

                                                                      For organizations just starting out or operating on very tight budgets, free membership management tools provide a viable entry point. Member.io offers a generous free tier that covers core member data management, basic communications, and simple dues tracking without a subscription commitment. 

                                                                      Key Features 

                                                                      • Member database with up to 250 contacts on the free plan 
                                                                      • Basic dues tracking and manual renewal management 
                                                                      • Email communications to members 
                                                                      • Simple event creation and attendance tracking 

                                                                            Reviews 

                                                                            Users appreciate the zero-cost entry point and ease of setup. The free tier is best suited for volunteer-run groups and early-stage organizations. Reviewers consistently note that organizations with growth ambitions will eventually outgrow the free plan's contact and feature limits. 

                                                                            Benefits 

                                                                            • Zero upfront cost to get started 
                                                                            • Minimal setup time for small volunteer-managed groups 
                                                                            • Upgrade path available as the organization grows 

                                                                                Pricing 

                                                                                Free for up to 250 contacts. Paid plans begin at approximately $25/month with expanded contact limits and additional automation features. 

                                                                                Choosing the Right Membership Management Software 

                                                                                Selecting the right membership management software comes down to three core considerations: the size and complexity of your organization, your membership model, and the integrations your operations depend on.  

                                                                                A small-staff association running a single chapter has fundamentally different needs than a national organization managing thousands of members across regional affiliates. 

                                                                                For organizations at the early stage or operating with limited staff, an all-in-one platform like YourMembership provides the simplicity and core functionality to run daily operations without overhead. Mid-size associations benefit from the operational depth of a platform like NetForum, while large and complex organizations—especially those with multi-chapter structures, advanced analytics needs, or enterprise integration requirements—are best served by Nimble AMS on Salesforce or Cobalt on Microsoft Dynamics 365. 

                                                                                Momentive Software's AMS portfolio is uniquely positioned to serve organizations at every point along that spectrum. Whether your association is just establishing its membership infrastructure or managing tens of thousands of members across a national network, Momentive offers a purpose-built solution—backed by 30+ years of AMS experience and a full ecosystem of complementary tools spanning fundraising, events, learning management, accounting, and certification.  

                                                                                For associations and membership organizations that need more than basic contact management, Momentive delivers the unified platform to grow, engage, and retain members at scale. 

                                                                                FAQ 

                                                                                What Is the Best Membership Management Software?

                                                                                Momentive AMS is ideal for comprehensive membership management. Smaller organizations may prefer Wild Apricot or MemberClicks, and nonprofits often choose Bloomerang for combined donor and member tracking. The best option depends on your organization’s size, model, and integration needs.

                                                                                What Is Membership Management Software Used For?

                                                                                It centralizes and automates membership operations, including member records, dues, communications, events, engagement tracking, and reporting—replacing spreadsheets and disconnected tools with a unified system for the full membership lifecycle.

                                                                                What Is the Difference Between Membership Management Software and a CRM?

                                                                                CRMs focus on sales and customer tracking, while membership management software is tailored for associations and nonprofits, handling dues, renewals, chapter management, benefits, and engagement. Using a CRM for membership often requires costly customization.

                                                                                What Features Should Membership Management Software Have?

                                                                                At a minimum, it should offer a member database, automated dues, event management, communication tools, and reporting. Advanced features include chapter management, accounting/fundraising integrations, self-service portals, and API access for custom workflows.

                                                                                How Much Does Membership Management Software Cost?

                                                                                Pricing varies by size and features. Small organizations may use free tiers; entry-level plans start at $25–$100/month. Mid-market platforms cost $300–$1,000+, while enterprise solutions like Fonteva or Momentive AMS are custom-priced. Free trials are usually available.
